title: SessionIssues
description: Root-level structured verifier-findings artifact for one findings-bearing verification round. Written beside sN-verification.md as sN-issues.json (round 1) or sN-issues-round-<M>.json (later findings-bearing retries). This artifact has no runtime reader in the orchestration layer; it is durable, machine-readable persistence of the verifier's structured issue list. The presence of an sN-issues*.json file means that verification round found issues — VERIFIED rounds get no file. See docs/session-issues-schema.md.

schemaVersion.description: Envelope schema version. 1 for the v1 contract locked in Set 055.

sessionNumber.description: 1-based session number within the set that this findings round belongs to.

verificationRound.description: 1-based verification round. Round 1 maps to sN-issues.json; round M>1 maps to sN-issues-round-<M>.json.

verificationVerdict.description: The verifier verdict for this round. Because a clean round writes no file, a persisted artifact is expected to carry a non-VERIFIED verdict; the field is preserved verbatim so the artifact is self-describing.

issues.description: The verifier's structured issue list for this round. At least one issue is present (the file would not exist otherwise).

Issue.description: One verifier finding. The verifier-emitted fields are preserved verbatim; description is the only reliable required field. category and severity are loose optional strings (the parser may emit 'unknown'). The resolution_* fields are optional, append-only, orchestrator-side annotations and are advisory only — they are NOT authoritative workflow state and no runtime gate consults them.

resolution_status.description: Advisory annotation: what the orchestrator did with this finding (e.g. 'fixed', 'deferred', 'wont-fix', 'false-positive').

resolution_notes.description: Advisory free-text annotation explaining the disposition of this finding.

resolved_in_round.description: Advisory annotation: the verification round in which this finding was resolved.
